nodes.label: AI as brainstorming and idea generator
nodes.label_negative: AI as sole decision-maker for creative work
nodes.grounding_span: treating AI as a colleague to bounce ideas off

nodes.label: AI as planning and structuring tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as creator of client-facing design
nodes.grounding_span: as an assistant for planning, strategising, helping with engaging content for social media

nodes.label: Authenticity in creative work
nodes.grounding_span: Authenticity really. As a creative person, I'm not comfortable selling something I haven't created

nodes.label: AI as decision-making support
nodes.grounding_span: It's a hugely valuable tool in providing options that I may not have thought of

nodes.label: Creative authenticity and human expression
nodes.grounding_span: I'm not comfortable selling something I haven't created

nodes.label: Business growth and profitability
nodes.grounding_span: how to grow the business, things I can do to get more traction in retail

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for brainstorming
nodes.grounding_span: treating AI as a colleague to bounce ideas off is for sure the most useful way

nodes.label: Resistance to AI in client communication
nodes.grounding_span: I definitely like to handle the design process myself, anything that is likely to end up with a client, I need to be sure it comes from me

nodes.label: Resistance to AI in creative work
nodes.grounding_span: I don't see it ever dictating what I do with my art

nodes.label: Collaborative iterative refinement — works back and forth with AI
nodes.grounding_span: I'll generally start with a few ideas... then we bounce ideas around

nodes.label: Domain boundary enforcement — clear separation between AI-assisted and human-only tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I actively make sure that I am striving to think of new ways to develop my work
